ocr.text: The study titled "Ferrari et al." conducted during the period 1983–1986 involved 127 patients (91 male/35 female) with a
median age of 15 (range 5–43), classified as Enneking stage IIB, and took place in Austria. "Bacci et al." covered the years
1986–1993 with 29 patients (25 male/4 female), a median age of 46, also in stage IIB, conducted in Italy. The "Edmonson et al."
study from 1976–1980 had 38 patients (1 male/37 female), a median age of 17 (range 9–62), classified as stage IIB, in Austria.
Research by "Gherlinzoni et al." from 1983–1988 included 355 patients (239 male/116 female) with no specified age or gender
data, falling under stage IIA, in Italy. The "Goorin et al." study spanning 1976–1989 reported 74 patients (36 male/38 female)
without median age data, classified as stage IIA, also in Austria.
Ref.  Study period  Patient number  Male/female  Median age (range)  Enneking stage  Country
Ferrari et al.  1983–1986  127 (91/35)  72/55  15 (5–43)  Stage IIB  Austria
Bacci et al.  1986–1993  29 (25/4)  20/9  46  Stage IIB  Italy
Edmonson et al.  1976–1980  38 (1/37)  24/14  17 (9–62)  Stage IIB  Austria
Gherlinzoni et al.  1983–1988  355 (239/116)  –  –  Stage IIA  Italy
Goorin et al.  1976–1989  74 (36/38)  –  –  Stage IIA  Austria
Zhang et al.  1981–1997  31 (17/14)  26/5  15 (7–42)  Stage IIB  China
Sha et al.  1989–2006  56 (35/21)  –  18 (2–46)  Stage IIA  China
Bramer et al.  1983–2003  56 (44/12)  36/20  16 (3–36)  Stage IIB  America
Ferguson et al.  1989–2006  31 (19/12)  14/17  30 (11–32)  Stage IIB  America
The "Zhang et al." study from 1981–1997 involved 31 patients (17 male/14 female) with a median age of 15 (range 7–42), in
stage IIB, conducted in China. "Sha et al." from 1989–2006 studied 56 patients (35 male/21 female), with a median age of 18
(range 2–46), in stage IIA, in China. "Niu et al." covered 1992–2001 with 189 patients (140 male/49 female) with a median age
of 18 (range 4–39), also in stage IIB, in China. The "Bramer et al." study from 1983–2003 comprised 56 patients (44 male/12
female), a median age of 16 (range 3–36), in stage IIB, in America. Lastly, "Ferguson et al." from 1989–2006 involved 31
patients (19 male/12 female) with a median age of 30 (range 11–32), in stage IIB, also in America.
